diagrams.description: This timeline traces the development of Art History as a discipline alongside major art historical periods. It begins with ancient art: cave paintings (c. 40,000 BCE), Classical Greek sculpture (5th‑4th cent. BCE), and Roman art (1st‑4th cent. CE). Medieval and Renaissance art (Byzantine mosaics, Giotto, Leonardo, Michelangelo) flourished before Giorgio Vasari’s ‘Lives of the Artists’ (1550), often considered the first art history text. The 18th century saw Johann Joachim Winckelmann’s ‘History of Ancient Art’ (1764) and the rise of Neoclassicism. The 19th century introduced Romanticism, Realism, Impressionism, and the formalist methods of Heinrich Wölfflin. The 20th century brought avant‑gardes (Cubism, Surrealism, Abstract Expressionism), the iconographic method (Panofsky’s ‘Studies in Iconology’, 1939), and feminist art history (Linda Nochlin’s ‘Why Have There Been No Great Women Artists?’, 1971). In recent decades, postcolonial and global art histories, the rise of contemporary art (globalisation, biennials, digital art), and technical art history have reshaped the field.
